

# =====================================================================
# Random Forest - Drug-Specific Models (Heroin, Cocaine, Meth)
# Using Full Predictors (Base + Demographics + Geography)
# =====================================================================
```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(randomForest)

# 1. טעינת הנתונים (הקובץ המלא הכולל גיאוגרפיה)
data_full <- read.csv("data/nsduh_clean_with_indexes_geo_id.csv")
# 2. הגדרת כלל המשתנים המסבירים שייכנסו למודלים
all_predictors <- c(
  "EDUHIGHCAT", "ECONOMIC_STATUS_INDEX", "EMPLOYMENT_INDEX", 
  "ECONOMIC_ASSISTANCE_INDEX", "MENTAL_HEALTH_INDEX", "IRINSUR4", # בסיס
  "CATAG3", "IRSEX", "IRMARIT",                                    # דמוגרפיה
  "COUTYP4", "PDEN10"                                             # גיאוגרפיה
)
```



# ==========================================
# MODEL 1: HEROIN USERS
# ==========================================
```{r}
rf_heroin_data <- data_full %>%
  filter(!is.na(Y_HEROIN)) %>%
  dplyr::select(Y_HEROIN, all_of(all_predictors)) %>%
  na.omit() %>%
  mutate(across(c(Y_HEROIN, CATAG3, IRSEX, IRMARIT, COUTYP4, PDEN10), as.factor))

min_class_heroin <- min(table(rf_heroin_data$Y_HEROIN))

set.seed(42)
model_heroin <- randomForest(
  Y_HEROIN ~ ., 
  data = rf_heroin_data,
  ntree = 500,
  importance = TRUE,
  sampsize = c(min_class_heroin, min_class_heroin) # איזון המודל
)

cat("\n--- HEROIN MODEL ---\n")
print(model_heroin)
varImpPlot(model_heroin, main = "Feature Importance: Heroin", pch = 19, color = "darkblue")
```



# ==========================================
# MODEL 2: COCAINE USERS
# ==========================================
```{r}
rf_cocaine_data <- data_full %>%
  filter(!is.na(Y_COCAINE)) %>%
  dplyr::select(Y_COCAINE, all_of(all_predictors)) %>%
  na.omit() %>%
  mutate(across(c(Y_COCAINE, CATAG3, IRSEX, IRMARIT, COUTYP4, PDEN10), as.factor))

min_class_cocaine <- min(table(rf_cocaine_data$Y_COCAINE))

set.seed(42)
model_cocaine <- randomForest(
  Y_COCAINE ~ ., 
  data = rf_cocaine_data,
  ntree = 500,
  importance = TRUE,
  sampsize = c(min_class_cocaine, min_class_cocaine) # איזון המודל
)

cat("\n--- COCAINE MODEL ---\n")
print(model_cocaine)
varImpPlot(model_cocaine, main = "Feature Importance: Cocaine", pch = 19, color = "darkgreen")
```



# ==========================================
# MODEL 3: METH USERS
# ==========================================
```{r}
rf_meth_data <- data_full %>%
  filter(!is.na(Y_METH)) %>%
  dplyr::select(Y_METH, all_of(all_predictors)) %>%
  na.omit() %>%
  mutate(across(c(Y_METH, CATAG3, IRSEX, IRMARIT, COUTYP4, PDEN10), as.factor))

min_class_meth <- min(table(rf_meth_data$Y_METH))

set.seed(42)
model_meth <- randomForest(
  Y_METH ~ ., 
  data = rf_meth_data,
  ntree = 500,
  importance = TRUE,
  sampsize = c(min_class_meth, min_class_meth) # איזון המודל
)

cat("\n--- METH MODEL ---\n")
print(model_meth)
varImpPlot(model_meth, main = "Feature Importance: Methamphetamine", pch = 19, color = "darkred")
```


# =====================================================================
# Model Evaluation: Heroin, Cocaine, and Methamphetamine
# =====================================================================
```{r}
# Load relevant libraries for model evaluation
library(caret)
library(pROC)
library(PRROC)

# Helper function for accurate calculation of the PR-AUC metric
get_pr_auc <- function(model, true_labels) {
  # Extract probabilities for the positive class ("1")
  probs <- predict(model, type = "prob")[, "1"]
  fg <- probs[true_labels == "1"] 
  bg <- probs[true_labels == "0"] 
  pr <- pr.curve(scores.class0 = fg, scores.class1 = bg, curve = FALSE)
  return(pr$auc.integral)
}

# --- 1. Heroin Model Evaluation ---
pred_heroin  <- predict(model_heroin)
cm_heroin    <- confusionMatrix(pred_heroin, rf_heroin_data$Y_HEROIN, positive = "1")
prob_heroin  <- predict(model_heroin, type = "prob")[, "1"]
auc_heroin   <- auc(roc(rf_heroin_data$Y_HEROIN, prob_heroin, quiet = TRUE))
prauc_heroin <- get_pr_auc(model_heroin, rf_heroin_data$Y_HEROIN)

# --- 2. Cocaine Model Evaluation ---
pred_cocaine <- predict(model_cocaine)
cm_cocaine   <- confusionMatrix(pred_cocaine, rf_cocaine_data$Y_COCAINE, positive = "1")
prob_cocaine <- predict(model_cocaine, type = "prob")[, "1"]
auc_cocaine  <- auc(roc(rf_cocaine_data$Y_COCAINE, prob_cocaine, quiet = TRUE))
prauc_cocaine <- get_pr_auc(model_cocaine, rf_cocaine_data$Y_COCAINE)

# --- 3. Methamphetamine Model Evaluation ---
pred_meth    <- predict(model_meth)
cm_meth      <- confusionMatrix(pred_meth, rf_meth_data$Y_METH, positive = "1")
prob_meth    <- predict(model_meth, type = "prob")[, "1"]
auc_meth     <- auc(roc(rf_meth_data$Y_METH, prob_meth, quiet = TRUE))
prauc_meth   <- get_pr_auc(model_meth, rf_meth_data$Y_METH)
```


# =====================================================================
# Create a central summary comparison table
# =====================================================================
```{r}
rf_models_evaluation <- data.frame(
  Model = c("1. Heroin", "2. Cocaine", "3. Methamphetamine"),
  Accuracy = c(cm_heroin$overall["Accuracy"], cm_cocaine$overall["Accuracy"], cm_meth$overall["Accuracy"]),
  Balanced_Accuracy = c(cm_heroin$byClass["Balanced Accuracy"], cm_cocaine$byClass["Balanced Accuracy"], cm_meth$byClass["Balanced Accuracy"]),
  Sensitivity = c(cm_heroin$byClass["Sensitivity"], cm_cocaine$byClass["Sensitivity"], cm_meth$byClass["Sensitivity"]),
  Specificity = c(cm_heroin$byClass["Specificity"], cm_cocaine$byClass["Specificity"], cm_meth$byClass["Specificity"]),
  F1_Score = c(cm_heroin$byClass["F1"], cm_cocaine$byClass["F1"], cm_meth$byClass["F1"]),
  ROC_AUC = c(auc_heroin, auc_cocaine, auc_meth),
  PR_AUC = c(prauc_heroin, prauc_cocaine, prauc_meth)
)

# Clean formatting and print the table with 3 decimal places
rownames(rf_models_evaluation) <- NULL
rf_models_evaluation[,-1] <- round(rf_models_evaluation[,-1], 3)

cat("\n=========================================================================\n")
cat("          Performance Comparison Table: Drug-Specific Models             \n")
cat("=========================================================================\n\n")
print(rf_models_evaluation)
```


```{r}
# 1. Create a unified comparison table
comparison_table <- data.frame(
  Drug = c("Heroin", "Cocaine", "Methamphetamine"),
  # Values from the Logistic Regression PDF
  Logistic_AUC = c(0.73, 0.649, 0.73), 
  # Values from the Random Forest models
  RF_AUC = c(auc_heroin, auc_cocaine, auc_meth) 
)

# 2. Display the table
print(comparison_table)

# 3. Create a comparative bar chart
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)

# Reshape data to long format for ggplot
comparison_long <- comparison_table %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(Logistic_AUC, RF_AUC), names_to = "Model_Type", values_to = "AUC")

# Generate the plot
ggplot(comparison_long, aes(x = Drug, y = AUC, fill = Model_Type)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Model Performance Comparison (AUC) by Drug Type",
       y = "Area Under the Curve (AUC)",
       x = "Drug Type",
       fill = "Model Strategy")
```
